steps.n: This is the basic form of the word that refers to a point in time. It's used to talk about when something happens.
Adding ا at the end makes it specifically about future events. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when the hypocrites come to the Prophet ﷺ.
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha marks
Added كَ at the end to show who is being come to (you)

steps.m: to pass through or spend something completely

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added alif after first letter to show pretending or doing something falsely
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action
Added ال to make it specific
Added ون to show multiple people
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. In this ayah, 'they' refers to the hypocrites (المنافقون) mentioned before.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb

steps.m: we testify (to رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ - that you are Allah's messenger)
steps.n: Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, changed vowels to show it's happening now, and added ُ at the end to complete the present tense form. This is what the hypocrites are saying to Muhammad ﷺ.
This is an emphatic particle that makes what comes after it more certain and adds emphasis

steps.m: indeed you (addressing the Prophet ﷺ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كَ (you) to show who is being addressed. In this ayah, it's addressing the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to emphasize that he is truly Allah's messenger.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed from a verb (to deliver) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added damma to show this is the subject of إِنَّكَ (indeed you)
Added لَ to emphasize that Muhammad is definitely Allah's messenger
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and greatness of Allah
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word. This word is connected to لَرَسُولُ (messenger) to mean 'messenger of Allah'.
This is a unique word that doesn't come from a root. It's the proper name for God in Arabic.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show Allah is doing the action (being the subject of 'testifies/bears witness')
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Changed alif to waṣla form since it comes after و
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense. In this ayah, Allah is the one who knows.

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes قَالُوا نَشْهَدُ - 'they said we testify')
steps.n: This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows. Here it emphasizes their testimony.
The كَ suffix adds the meaning of 'you' (masculine singular). This word connects to نَشْهَدُ (we testify) - they are testifying about the Prophet ﷺ.
This is a suffix pronoun that attaches to words to mean 'you' or 'your'
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
The ل at the start adds emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'
Added ُ (damma) to show this is the main subject of the sentence
Added هُۥ to show this messenger belongs to Allah. This word completes the statement that begins with إِنَّكَ (indeed you are)
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) at the end to show Allah is doing the action (knowing) in this sentence

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part with what came before it in the verse
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening in the present. Changed vowels to match present tense pattern.
This is the basic conditional particle that forms the base of the emphatic particle
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional particle to an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Muhammad is truly Allah's messenger.
The root represents the concept of spending or using up

steps.m: to show one thing while hiding its opposite
steps.n: Added ا between first and second root letters to show the back-and-forth nature of the action - showing one thing openly while hiding another

steps.m: one who shows one thing while hiding another
steps.n: Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to create a noun meaning 'one who does this action'

steps.m: people who show one thing while hiding another
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one person)

steps.m: the specific people who show one thing while hiding another
steps.n: Added definite article ال to make the word definite
Added a small diagonal line (fatha) at the end because إِنَّ came before this word and makes it take this mark
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changes verb to describe someone who does the action
Makes it plural to show multiple liars
Adds لَ to emphasize that they are definitely lying
This is the basic root meaning of taking or grasping something
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The ت is doubled (has shadda) to show extra effort in the action
Added وا at the end to change from 'he took' to 'they took', showing multiple people did the action

steps.m: to make an oath or promise
steps.n: The root relates to the right side or being blessed
Changes from single oath to multiple oaths by adding أ at the start and ا in the middle - like going from 'promise' to 'promises'
The word gets a fatha at the end because it's the object (thing being taken) in the phrase 'they took their oaths'
Added هُمْ to show the oaths belong to them
The root means to cover or hide something
Added damma (u sound) and doubled the middle letter to change from 'to protect' into 'protection'
Added ة to show one specific instance of protection
Added tanween (ً) to show this is the object being taken in the phrase 'they took their oaths as a shield'
Added shadda on middle letter to show emphasis, with fatha on first letter to make it past tense
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action

steps.m: so they blocked/turned away (from سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this action happened as a result of something before it. In this ayah, they are turning away from 'the path of Allah'
This is a preposition that shows blocking or turning away from something. In this ayah, it connects with سَبِيلِ (path) to show they blocked others from Allah's path.
Root meaning relates to path or way
Adding vowel marks to show it's a temporary path or way
Added ي to make it mean a permanent path/way rather than a temporary one
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, meaning 'path of Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it specific and the shadda emphasizes its importance. The kasra at the end shows it's being acted upon in this sentence.
إِنَّ is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed they (subject of سَآءَ - how evil is)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end which means 'they' or 'them'. This word connects to سَآءَ (how evil is) in the next ayah, showing who did the evil action.
Root meaning of being bad or evil

steps.m: it was evil (describes what they did)
steps.n: Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'it was evil/bad'. Like saying something 'turned out badly'.

steps.m: it was evil (describes what they did)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: it was evil (describes what they did)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's a long vowel. This doesn't change the meaning, just the writing.
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/used to do) in this ayah. It introduces a clause describing what was being done.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The weak middle letter و changes to ا, which is very common for hollow verbs
Added وا to show multiple people (they) did the action. This word is the subject of يَعْمَلُونَ (they do/practice) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they do/perform (referring to the hypocrites mentioned in إِنَّهُمْ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers back to the hypocrites mentioned earlier with إِنَّهُمْ (indeed they)
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this'
Added لِ to give emphasis and extend the pointing meaning
Added كَ at the end to show distance, changing 'this' to 'that'
This is a special word that introduces emphasis or certainty to what follows
Adding بِ at the beginning changes 'that' to 'because' - it shows the reason for something
Adding هُمْ shows who the word is talking about - in this case, it refers to those who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا۟) later in the verse

steps.m: to trust or feel secure in something

steps.n: Adding alif after first letter makes it mean 'to have faith' instead of just 'to feel secure'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added the small circle (۟) above وا to show it's a plural marker
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happened after another with a gap in time. Here it connects their believing (ءَامَنُوا۟) with their later disbelieving (كَفَرُوا۟).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense singular form
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to فَطُبِعَ (so it was sealed) which explains what happened as a result of their disbelief.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: it was sealed (subject of فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ)
steps.n: The vowel changes make this passive - showing something received the action rather than did it. This word connects to فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ (so they do not understand) by explaining why they don't understand.

steps.m: so it was sealed (subject of فَهُمْ لَا يَفْقَهُونَ)
steps.n: Adding فَ (so/then) shows this sealing was a result of what came before - they believed then disbelieved, so their hearts were sealed
This is the basic form of the preposition, showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition showing something is on top of something else. Here it connects with قُلُوبِهِمْ (their hearts) to show something is upon their hearts.
The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
The root meaning of turning/changing becomes 'heart' since the heart is always turning and changing emotions. This is the basic noun form.
Changes the word to show many hearts by changing the vowels inside the word and adding و

steps.m: their hearts (object of طُبِعَ عَلَىٰ - sealed upon)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show these hearts belong to them. In this ayah, their hearts are being sealed (طُبِعَ عَلَىٰ).
Basic pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' (referring to those who disbelieved)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what happened before. This connects to the next phrase 'they do not understand'.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَفْقَهُونَ (they understand) into 'they do not understand'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning and changed vowels to show present tense. The sukun (ْ) helps pronunciation.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not understand'
This is a conditional particle that shows when something happens. It usually connects two actions - when the first thing happens, the second thing follows.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
The root letter ي changes to ى at the end of the word
Changed the root letters to past tense by adding vowels (َ) and writing with ى at the end
The final ى changes back to يْ before adding suffixes
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was seen ('them')
This is the basic concept of being amazed or impressed by something
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ prefix
Added كَ at the end to show the person being amazed is 'you'
Adds vowels to make the root into a word for a physical thing
Changes from singular 'body' to plural 'bodies' by adding أَ at the start and ا after س - this is a broken plural pattern
Adds ُ to show these bodies are doing something - they are amazing/impressing you (تُعْجِبُكَ) in this verse
Adds هُمْ at end to show these are the bodies belonging to the hypocrites being discussed

steps.m: if (introduces condition for يَقُولُوا۟ - they say)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation - 'if they say something, you will hear...'

steps.m: and if (introduces condition for يَقُولُوا۟ - they say)
steps.n: Adds وَ at start to connect this conditional phrase with the previous statement about their bodies impressing you
Added fathas to make the past tense verb. The middle و changes to ا in this form.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to hear or listen to something

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he heard'
Changed from 'he heard' to 'you hear' by adding تَ (ta) at start to show 'you' and changing vowels to show present time

steps.m: you hear/listen (in context of إِن - if you listen)
steps.n: Changed final vowel to sukun because it follows إِن (if) in the ayah
Changes from verb to noun meaning 'speech'
Adds ِ to show this word is possessed by something or follows certain words like لِ
هِمْ means 'their' and shows who did the saying

steps.m: because of their speech (reason for تَسْمَعْ - you listen)
steps.n: لِ here means 'because of' and shows why the action تَسْمَعْ (you listen) happens
Starting with the basic comparison particle
Combines كَ (like) with أَنَّ (that) to mean 'as if' - makes comparisons more emphatic

steps.m: as if they (are like خُشُبٌ مُّسَنَّدَةٌ/pieces of wood propped up)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this describes 'them' - the hypocrites who are being compared to pieces of wood propped up
The root transforms into a plural noun meaning 'pieces of wood'
Adding damma vowels creates a plural form pattern that shows multiple pieces of something
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it indefinite - meaning 'some pieces of wood'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic noun
Added مُ at the beginning to show something is receiving the action, and doubled the ن to add emphasis - like a chair that is being propped up
Added ة at the end to make it feminine - like describing a feminine object that is propped up
Added double dammah to make it indefinite - like saying 'a propped up thing' instead of 'the propped up thing'
This is the basic concept of calculating, thinking, or considering something
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and changing last vowel to ُ
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a noun that shows totality or completeness
Added damma and shadda to make it into a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object (thing being counted) in relation to يَحْسَبُونَ (they think/count)
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form a basic noun
Adds -ah ending to show it was one single shout (like saying 'a shout' vs 'shouting')

steps.m: a single loud shout/cry (being counted as one of many things they fear)
steps.n: Adds a mark at the end to show it's connected to the word 'every' (كُلَّ) in the sentence
This is a preposition showing position or relationship. It's one of the basic prepositions in Arabic that doesn't come from a root.
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for the pronoun attachment
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to a group of men or mixed group (them). This word appears in an ayah describing the hypocrites.
Basic form of the independent pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' for a group of males or mixed group
Added damma (ُ) at the end because this word connects to the next word العَدُوُّ (the enemy). Together they form the sentence 'They are the enemy'

steps.m: to be hostile, to overstep bounds

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic word for someone who is hostile
Added a doubled و (shadda) to show it's a strong/intense enemy
Added the definite article 'the'
Added damma at the end because this word is the subject (predicate) of هُمُ (they are) in this ayah

steps.m: to be on guard against danger

steps.n: Changed root letters into a command by adding vowel marks - telling someone to be careful!
Added helper alif to make the command easier to pronounce
Added هُمْ to show who to be on guard against

steps.m: so be on guard against them! (because of what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result of what was mentioned before in the ayah about their deceptive appearance
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The alif shows two people are fighting each other, not just one person attacking
هُمْ means 'them' - shows who received the action

steps.m: fought against them (object of قٰتَلَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ - Allah fought against them)
steps.n: The هُمْ (them) was already there in both words - it wasn't added. The only change is the alif was written as a long ā (ٰ). The word means 'fought against them' and is part of the phrase 'Allah fought against them'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed from the general word for 'god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال, merging letters together, and doubling the ل. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing the action
This is the base interrogative particle
Adding ى with alif maksura (ىٰ) changes this from 'that' to a question word asking about manner. Here it's questioning how they are being turned away from the truth.

steps.m: to deceive, turn away from truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form past tense
Changed from past tense 'turned away' to present tense 'is turned away' by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are being turned away
This is a conditional particle that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when something is said to the hypocrites.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي to complete passive voice pattern
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning
Added the pronoun هُمْ (them) to show who the action is directed towards. This word connects to يَسْتَغْفِرْ (seek forgiveness) in the ayah, showing who the forgiveness is for.

steps.m: to rise, be high, or elevated

steps.n: The تَ and ا work together to show people doing something as a group, changing 'be high' into 'come forward' (since coming to someone involves raising yourself and moving)

steps.m: all of you come! (command to a group)
steps.n: Adding وْا makes this a command to multiple people

steps.m: all of you come! (command to a group)
steps.n: Added a small circle (۟) above the word which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to forgive, pardon, or cover faults

steps.n: Adding the 3 short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) turns the root into 'he forgave' in the past tense
Adding است at the beginning changes the meaning from doing the action to seeking or requesting it
Adding ي at start shows this is happening in the present tense
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes from a verb (sending) to a noun (person who sends/delivers)
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it a unique name for The One God
Added shadda on ل and kasra at the end to show this is describing whose messenger (رَسُولُ) this is

steps.m: to turn away, twist, or bend

steps.n: Added shadda (double mark) on و to show the action is done with force or intensity, matching the context of defiant rejection in the ayah

steps.m: they turned away defiantly (subject of يَصُدُّونَ)
steps.n: Added وْا to show multiple people did this action. This word connects to يَصُدُّونَ (they turn away) later in the ayah, showing their continuous rejection.

steps.m: head (the body part) or to be chief/leader

steps.n: Added vowel marks to the root letters to make the word mean 'a head'
Changed the word pattern to make it plural. The أ changes to ء and we add ُو after it

steps.m: heads (as object of لَوَّوْا۟ - they turned)
steps.n: Added fatHa to show this is the object of the verb لَوَّوْا۟ (they turned)

steps.m: their heads (as object of لَوَّوْا۟ - they turned)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show these heads belong to them (the hypocrites mentioned in the verse)
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make the past tense form

steps.m: and you saw (while يَصُدُّونَ/they turn away)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the following action of them turning away

steps.m: and you saw them (while يَصُدُّونَ/they turn away)
steps.n: Added هُمْ (them) to show who was being seen - refers to the hypocrites mentioned earlier who turn away
Added shadda on middle letter to show intensity of action, with fatha on first letter to show past tense
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start
Adds plural ending to show multiple people doing the action (in this case, the hypocrites mentioned in the ayah)
This is a third person masculine plural pronoun that acts as the subject of the verb يَصُدُّونَ (they turn away) in this ayah
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the ayah, showing these actions happen together
Basic past tense showing a quality or characteristic
Added letters to show someone who deliberately makes themselves seem greater than others
Added ending to show this refers to multiple people
Added emphasis to the م and a final vowel. This word describes the people who are turning away (يَصُدُّونَ) in the verse.

steps.m: to be level or the same

steps.n: Root letters got vowels (fatha) and alif at end to make a word meaning 'being equal'
Added a long alif and hamza at the end to turn it from a verb into a word meaning 'equal' or 'same'
Added double damma marks to show this is an indefinite noun in the nominative case
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns at the end. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
هِمْ shows this refers to a group of people being talked about (them). In this ayah, it refers to the hypocrites mentioned earlier.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show asking for or seeking forgiveness
Changed to show 'you' (one male person) did this action in the past. The ت at the end shows it's 'you' and the أ at the start is a spelling change.
This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose. Here it connects with the verb يَغْفِرَ (forgive) to show who is being forgiven.
Added هُمْ to show this refers to multiple people (them). In this ayah, it refers to the hypocrites being discussed.

steps.m: or (connecting the two options: whether you ask forgiveness or don't)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects two equal choices in a question. Here it connects 'whether you ask forgiveness' with 'or don't ask forgiveness', showing both options lead to the same result.

This is a negative particle that makes verbs negative in the past, like saying 'did not' in English. In this ayah, it negates تَسْتَغْفِرْ (you seek forgiveness), making it 'you did not seek forgiveness'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adds reflexive meaning - seeking/requesting forgiveness
Adding ت at the start changes it from 'he sought' to 'you seek' forgiveness
This is a pronoun meaning 'them' (referring to multiple males). It's being used as the object of the verb أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ (I asked forgiveness).
The لَ prefix shows this is being done 'for' or 'to' them. This word is connected to the verb أَسْتَغْفَرْتَ (I asked forgiveness), showing who the forgiveness was asked for.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يَغْفِرَ) mean 'will not' happen in the future. In this ayah, it changes 'will forgive' to 'will never forgive'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future
Changed the ending vowel to fatha to show this is in the subjunctive mood
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the unique name of the one true God by adding ال, doubling the ل with shadda, and adding ُ to show this is the subject of the verb يَهْدِى (guides) in this ayah
This is a basic preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it shows who you're asking forgiveness for.
Added هُمْ to show it refers to 'them' (masculine plural), and fatha (َ) on ل to connect the letters. This shows who you're asking forgiveness for in the verb تَسْتَغْفِرْ (you seek forgiveness).
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that strengthens what comes after it

steps.m: indeed (emphasizing that اللَّهَ will not guide)
steps.n: Adding the shadda makes the emphasis stronger. This particle introduces and emphasizes the statement that follows about Allah not guiding the disobedient people.
Changed the word to the special form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَغْفِرَ - will forgive)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being acted upon in this sentence
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates the verb يَهْدِى (guides), making it mean 'does not guide'.
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
This is the basic concept of showing someone the right way
Added basic past tense vowels. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to ِ, and added sukun after first letter to help pronunciation
Added ى at the end to show it's a present tense verb

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up
a group of people (who stand together)
steps.n: Changed root meaning of standing into a noun meaning 'people' - like how we call a group that stands together a 'standing committee'
Added ال at beginning to mean 'the' and َ at end to show this word receives the action of guiding
This form shows who does the action. Added missing vowel marks to show correct pronunciation.

steps.m: people who break rules/disobey (3 or more males)
steps.n: The ين ending changes the word from one disobedient person to many disobedient people (3 or more males)

steps.m: the people who break rules/disobey (object of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: The ال at the start means 'the'. This word is receiving the action of يَهْدِى (guides) - Allah does not guide these people.
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to a group of people (usually male or mixed gender). It doesn't come from a root since it's one of Arabic's core pronouns.

steps.m: they (subject of يَقُولُونَ - those who say)
steps.n: Added damma marks (ُ) to show this is the subject form of the pronoun. This word connects to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in the ayah, showing who is doing the saying.

steps.m: who/which (connects a description to one thing)
steps.n: This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to things or people

steps.m: the one who (connects a description to one thing)
steps.n: Added ال with a shadda to make it more specific

steps.m: those who (connects a description to people or things)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word connects to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form, which is the base for other forms
Changes from past tense 'said' to present tense 'says' by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they say (referring to the hypocrites mentioned earlier in the ayah)
steps.n: Adds plural ending to show multiple people are speaking. In this ayah, it refers to the hypocrites (المنافقين) mentioned later
This is a negative word that makes the command after it (تُنفِقُوا۟ meaning 'spend') into a negative command ('do not spend'). It's like adding 'don't' before a verb in English.
Connected the root letters with short 'a' vowels (fatHa) to make 'he spent' in the past
Added hamza at beginning to show causing someone else to do the action
Changed from 'he made spend' to 'you spend' by adding تُ
Added damma at end to show regular present tense
Changes from singular 'you spend' to plural 'you all spend'
Added plural marker to show multiple people are doing the action
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes it from a concept to a word showing relationship between things

steps.m: upon/on (shows position above or about something else)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the long vowel sound
This word is being used to mean 'whoever' in this context, referring to the people that the hypocrites are telling others not to spend on. It is the object of the verb تُنفِقُوا (spend on).
Adding kasra on first letter turns this into a word showing location or closeness to something
Adding fatha at the end shows this word connects to the word after it (رَسُولِ meaning messenger)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
This word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah) which comes after it, showing this is Allah's messenger
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the proper name of the One God. The ِ at the end shows this word belongs to or relates to something else in the sentence.
This is the base form of the particle that shows when something ends
Adding ى at the end helps connect this word to the next verb (يَنفَضُّوا۟) to show the endpoint of an action
The small alif (ٰ) above the ى is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed middle vowel to make it mean 'became scattered' instead of 'scattered something'
Added يَ at start to show 'he' doing it now, and ن to show 'breaking apart' by itself, with a stronger ضّ sound
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in the ayah.
Added لِ at the beginning to show something belongs or relates to Allah, and ِ at the end to connect it to other words
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This phrase tells us that the treasures (خَزَآئِنُ) belong to Allah.

steps.m: to store or protect something valuable

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense meaning 'he stored'
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to make it mean 'one who does this action'
Changed the pattern to make it plural, showing multiple places where things are stored
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject. The alif is written as آ for style but pronounced the same.

steps.m: to rise up, be elevated, or go high

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high above
Adding ال makes the word definite
Doubling the م makes the meaning stronger
Changes from singular 'sky/heaven' to plural 'heavens' - like going from 'sky' to 'skies'. The word is connected to 'treasures' in the phrase 'treasures of the heavens'.
Adds kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by خَزَآئِنُ (treasuries) in the phrase 'treasuries of the heavens'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added و at the beginning to connect it to the previous word (السماوات)

steps.m: and the earth/land (possessed by خَزَآئِنُ - treasures)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this is possessed by خَزَآئِنُ (treasures), forming 'treasures of the earth'
This is the basic particle that shows contrast with what came before. The alif after ل is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this case.
Adding shadda and fatha at the end makes the word stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' to its meaning
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this contrasting statement to what came before it
The added ا shows the person is pretending or acting falsely with others
مُ at start shows this describes a person who does this action
Changed from singular (one hypocrite) to plural (many hypocrites) by adding ين at the end. The ِ vowel shows it's being affected by another word
The word gets 'the' in front (ال) to make it specific hypocrites
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تُنفِقُوا۟ (spend) into 'do not spend'. In this ayah, it's part of what some people are saying - they're telling others not to spend.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Added ون to show multiple people. This word is negated by لا (not) in the ayah.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. The middle و changes to ا which is common with this type of word.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle letter returns to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple men are doing the action
Added fatha to show this is the emphasis لَ

steps.m: surely if (introduces a conditional statement in يَقُولُونَ they say)
steps.n: Added إِنْ (if) to create a compound particle that introduces a conditional statement with emphasis. This particle is connected to يَقُولُونَ (they say) and introduces what they are saying.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Doubling the ج makes the verb mean 'to cause to return' instead of just 'return'
This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction towards something. In this ayah, it connects 'we returned' (رَّجَعْنَآ) with 'the city' (ٱلْمَدِينَةِ) to show where they returned to.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: kasra (ِ) under the إ and fatha (َ) above the ل
Adding vowels creates the basic past tense verb
Added ي and ة to make it mean 'city' - a place where people settle
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific - 'the city'
Added kasra at the end to show movement towards or connection to the city
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changes from 'he left' to 'he makes someone leave' by adding yu- at start
Adds emphasis/certainty to the verb by adding لَ at start

steps.m: surely he will expel (referring to the hypocrites' claim about expelling believers from Medina)
steps.n: Added heavy emphasis نَّ which shows strong assertion about a future action
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative, meaning 'more' or '-er'
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: the mightier one (contrasted with ٱلْأَذَلَّ - the more humiliated one)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to point to a specific mighty one, contrasting with its opposite mentioned later in the verse
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced. مِنْ is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere else.

steps.m: from it/her (referring to الْمَدِينَةِ - the city)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to الْمَدِينَةِ (the city) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to comparative form (more/most) - like saying 'lower' or 'weaker' compared to something else

steps.m: the more humble one (object of لَيُخْرِجَنَّ)
steps.n: This word is the object (receiver) of the verb لَيُخْرِجَنَّ (he will surely expel) in this ayah. The fatha at the end shows it's receiving the action.
This is the base word for God's name in Arabic, with shadda on the ل to emphasize it
Added لِ at the beginning to show something belongs or is directed to Allah. The ا from الله is dropped when لِ is added.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: and for/to Allah (connected to الْعِزَّةُ - honor/might)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with the word الْعِزَّةُ (honor/might) that comes before it in the ayah
Added kasra on first letter and shadda on second letter to make the basic noun form
Added ة to show one specific instance of might
Added ال to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the subject, and changed اَلْ to ٱلْ which is how it's written in the Quran
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Adding conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Adding preposition prefix ل meaning 'for/to'

steps.m: and to/for His messenger (referring to Allah's messenger)
steps.n: Added possessive pronoun هِۦ meaning 'His' - here referring to Allah's messenger. The ۦ mark shows this is a special pronunciation in the Quran.

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, be secure

steps.n: Added long alif at beginning changes 'feeling safe' into 'believing/having faith' - like going from feeling safe yourself to trusting in something bigger
Added مُ at start to show 'one who' and changed middle vowels to make it mean 'the person who believes'
Adds sound masculine plural ending ين
Added لِلْ (for the) at beginning
Added و conjunction prefix meaning 'and'
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast with what came before
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with what came before
Added vowel marks and lengthened alif to help with pronunciation
Added emphasis at the end to strengthen the contrast with what came before

steps.m: to spend or use up resources
to act two-faced (pretending one thing while doing another)
steps.n: Added alif between first and second letters to show the meaning of doing something in a two-faced way
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show this describes someone who does this action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple hypocrites

steps.m: the hypocrites (subject of لا يعلمون - they do not know)
steps.n: Added ال at start to make it specific. These hypocrites are the ones who 'do not know' in the next part of the verse.
This is the basic negative particle that negates the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) that comes after it
Added fatha mark on ل to show proper pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future. The ْ (sukun) on ع shows there's no vowel there.
Added ون to show many people are doing this. This word relates to يَقُولُونَ (they say) in the ayah - it's the same group of people doing both actions.
Basic root meaning
The middle letter is doubled to make the questioning stronger
The ُ shows this word is the main thing being talked about
Added يَٰٓ at the start to call out to someone, with a long sound to show emphasis
Added ها at the end to point to who is being called out to
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال (the) at the start to make it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) which comes right after it.
Added alif after first letter to intensify the meaning
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb تُلْهِكُمْ (distract you) into 'do not let distract you'. It's used here as a prohibition.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to be distracted or diverted from something important
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Adds the basic vowel pattern to make it a readable word
Changed to present tense form. The word أَمْوٰلُكُمْ (your wealth) is feminine singular, so it uses the feminine form of the verb.

steps.m: it distracts you all (from ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being distracted. In this ayah, it's warning about wealth distracting people from remembering Allah.
Basic root meaning
The root letters combine to make a noun, with the middle letter و changing to ا (alif) - a common change in Arabic words
Added أ at the start and changed to plural by adding و to make many properties/wealth
Added tanween dammah to show this is any wealth/properties (not specific ones)
The damma shows this word is the subject or after certain particles
Added كُمْ (kum) at the end to mean 'your' for multiple people
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Basic root meaning
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changes from 'to give birth' (verb) to 'children' (plural noun) - a common plural form for child/offspring
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an alif khanjariya
Adds كُمْ to show possession by multiple people being addressed

steps.m: away from (separates ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ from تُلْهِكُمْ)
steps.n: This is a helper word that shows something is being separated or distanced from something else. Here it shows that wealth and children shouldn't distract you away from remembering Allah.
Basic root meaning
Changed the verb into a noun by adding ِ and ْ. This makes it mean the actual act or result of remembering, like how 'write' becomes 'writing'.
Added ِ at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes right after it in the ayah, forming the phrase 'remembrance of Allah'
Changed to the specific name for Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The first ل gets a shadda because it combines with the ل from ال.
Added ِ at the end to show this belongs to 'remembrance of' (ذِكْرِ). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Combination of 'and' with relative pronoun meaning 'whoever'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he does/performs (in conditional phrase with مَن)
steps.n: Changed ending to sukun because it comes after مَن (whoever) in a conditional phrase
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show elongation
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
The كَ is added at the end to complete the demonstrative pronoun
Added ئِكَ to show we're pointing to something far away, like saying 'those over there' in English

Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what was said before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about those who let their wealth and children distract them from remembering Allah.
Basic pronoun meaning 'they' (masculine plural)
The sukun (ْ) changes to damma (ُ) because it comes before ٱلْخٰسِرُونَ (the losers). This helps connect the words smoothly when speaking.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from verb (to lose) to noun meaning 'loser'
Added plural ending to show multiple people

steps.m: the losers (those who are the real losers)
steps.n: Added 'the' to show these are specific losers being referred to in this context

steps.m: to spend or give away resources

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it about causing or making something happen
Changed from 'he spent' (past) to 'I spend' (present) by changing the middle vowel from 'a' to 'i' and adding 'u' at the end
Adding وا makes it a command for multiple people
Adding وَ at start connects this command to what came before it
This is a preposition showing where something starts from. In this ayah, it comes before مَّا (what) to show 'from what' Allah has provided you.
This is the basic form of the word ما which is used to refer to things (not people). In this ayah, it connects رَزَقْنٰكُم (We provided you) to مِن (from), showing what was provided.

steps.m: what/that which (object of رَزَقْنٰكُم - We provided you)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after مِن, which often causes the next word to start with a doubled letter for easier pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added sukun to prepare for attachments
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added كُم to show the action was done to 'you all' (plural)
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added shadda (ّ) on م to make the sound stronger. This happens because the word before it ends with a similar sound.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Changes from verb meaning to noun meaning of 'before'
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to أَن (that) which comes after it. Together they mean 'before that' in the ayah.
This is a connecting word that introduces what will happen - in this case, it connects to the verb يَأْتِىَ (comes/approaches). It's like saying 'before that death comes' in English.
This is the basic concept of coming or arriving
Added يَ at the start to show it's happening now and 'he' is doing it. The ْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) help with pronunciation.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to complete the word's basic form
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic noun meaning 'one'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon

steps.m: one of you (object of يَأْتِىَ - comes to)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show this refers to 'you all'. This word is the object of يَأْتِىَ (comes to) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun for death
Added the definite article 'al' to specify this particular death
Added the ُ (damma) ending to show this word is the subject doing the action of 'coming' in the phrase 'death comes'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to common past tense form by adding alif after ق
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: and then he says (as a result of what came before)
steps.n: Added فَ (meaning 'and then/so') at start and changed the ending to َ to show this is a result of the previous action
Connected the root letters and added a shadda (double letter) to show intensity - meaning someone who nurtures completely
Adding kasra at the end makes it a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
This is the first part of the compound particle expressing a conditional meaning

steps.m: if only (expressing regret in فَيَقُولَ - so he will say)
steps.n: Adding لَا changes the meaning from a simple 'if' to express regret or wishful thinking. In this ayah, it shows the regret of someone when death comes to them.
The doubled خ shows that this is not just a simple delay, but an emphasized or repeated delay
The middle letter خ is doubled with a shadda (ّ) to show doing something with extra force or making someone else do it
The suffix 'تَ' changes the subject of the verb to 'you' (masculine singular).
The suffix 'نِ' adds an object pronoun, meaning 'me'.  It shows who was affected by the action of delaying.

steps.m: you delayed me (object of فَيَقُولَ - so he says)
steps.n: The ي (yaa) at the end shows 'me' as the object, with a long vowel sound
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to أَجَلٍ (appointed term) to show 'to an appointed term'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: kasra under إ and fatha over ل
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif on top) at the end to show this is how the word is written in the Quran
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) above the final letter to show a slight lengthening in recitation
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to create the basic noun form
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (like saying 'an appointed time' instead of 'the appointed time'). This word comes after the word إِلَىٰٓ (to) in the ayah.
This pattern creates an adjective describing the quality of being near
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective describing the quality of being near
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of the word إِلَىٰٓ (to) in the phrase 'to a near term'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he gave charity (demonstrated truth through action)
steps.n: Adding shadda intensifies the meaning and transforms it from just being truthful to actively demonstrating truth through charitable giving
Adding أ at start makes it 'I will' do the action

steps.m: so I give charity (connected to أَخَّرْتَنِي - you delayed me)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result of the previous action (being given more time). This connects to the previous word أَخَّرْتَنِي (you delayed me).
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at start to make 'I' form of the verb

steps.m: and I be (connected to فَأَصَّدَّقَ - so I give charity)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the previous verb فَأَصَّدَّقَ (so I give charity). Together they express 'so I can give charity and be...'
This is a preposition that shows where something starts from or what group it belongs to. Think of it like pointing to where something came from.
The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger, which happens when certain letters come before it
The fatha (َ) at the end connects this word to the next word الصَّالِحِينَ (the righteous), showing it means 'from the righteous'
Added ا after first letter and ِ on second letter to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people. The ِ in ين shows this comes after the word مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah.
Added ٱل at the beginning to mean 'the'. The ل combines with ص to make a stronger ص sound (shown by ّ). Added َ at the end because it comes after وَأَكُن (and I will be) in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that strongly negates future actions. In this ayah, it negates the verb يُؤَخِّرَ (will delay), making it mean 'will never delay'.

steps.m: and never/will not (negates يُؤَخِّرَ - will delay)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English.

steps.m: to put something back or make it late

steps.n: Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing it's not just a simple delay but a more intense or deliberate one
Changed to present tense and added subjunctive ending because it comes after لَن (will not). The word لَن makes it clear this won't happen in the future.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' or 'something worshipped'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity
The U mark at the end shows this word is the subject of the sentence
The pattern creates a noun meaning 'soul' or 'self' from the basic idea of breathing/being alive

steps.m: a soul (object of يُؤَخِّرَ - will delay)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of delay - Allah will not delay any soul
This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it connects 'when' with 'their time comes' (جَآءَ أَجَلُهَا)
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he came' by adding fatha vowels. Shows something happening before its time/deadline (أَجَلُهَا) in this ayah.
Changed to common past tense form by adding alif. This is how 'come' is normally written in Arabic.

steps.m: to delay or set a time limit

steps.n: Adding the basic vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb

steps.m: a set time/deadline (subject of جَآءَ - came)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (delayed) to a noun (deadline) and added ُ to show it's the subject doing the action of 'coming'

steps.m: its appointed time (subject of جَآءَ - came)
steps.n: Added هَا which means 'its' or 'her', referring back to نَفْسًا (a soul) mentioned earlier. This whole word is doing the action of 'coming' in the phrase 'when its appointed time comes'
This is the base form of Allah's name. Unlike most Arabic words, it doesn't come from a three-letter root because it's a unique divine name.
Added shadda to show the doubled ل sound, which is special to this divine name.

steps.m: Allah (as the doer of يُؤَخِّرَ - will delay)
steps.n: Added damma to show that Allah is the subject (doer) of the action in this sentence. Here, Allah is the one who will not delay a soul when its time comes.

steps.m: And Allah (as the doer of يُؤَخِّرَ - will delay)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this part to what came before. Also added the helper alif (ٱ) which is a writing convention.

steps.m: to inform, be aware of details

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'news' from the basic idea of 'informing'

steps.m: one who is thoroughly aware (describing اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Changed from 'news' to 'someone who knows all news/information' by adding an 'i' sound in the middle
The tanween shows this is one of Allah's attributes being used as a name
This is a compound of the preposition 'بِ' (with) and the relative pronoun 'مَا' (what)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he worked' to 'you work' by adding تَ at the start
The ون ending shows that multiple people are being addressed. This verb describes what is being done with ما (what/that which) in the ayah.
